competitor_patterns: Massive word counts (16,000–24,000 words) with exhaustive multi-brand comparisons, buyer guides, and tested-product reviews — far exceeding our 1,073–1,325 word posts
Structured comparison tables are standard (Outdoorgearlab, Bikerumor both use them) with specs side-by-side: weight, lens tech, price, interchangeability — we only added one table in one post
Segmented H2 categories targeting sub-audiences: 'Best MTB,' 'Best Road Bike Glasses,' 'Best Value,' 'Best on a Tight Budget' — allowing one page to capture multiple long-tail queries simultaneously
FAQ sections are near-universal in top-3 content, driving featured snippets and AI Overview citations — we have FAQs but they are brief and not optimised for conversational queries

Top competitors emphasise real-world testing credibility ('We tested 18 pairs,' 'Tested & Ranked') in both title tags and meta descriptions, which directly boosts CTR from searchers who distrust brand content
our_gaps: Word count is 10–20x below top competitors: our posts average ~1,200 words vs 9,735–24,529 for ranking pages — Google rewards depth and topical completeness for 'best of' queries
No comparison tables in most posts: the 'Budget vs Premium' and 'Endurasport' posts have zero tables, missing the structured data format that earns featured snippets for spec-heavy queries

Posts are brand-first (Velluto StradaPro appears in H2s early) rather than problem-first and multi-brand: top rankers name 5–10 competitors before recommending a winner, building trust and capturing more query variants

We have zero content targeting the highest-impression keyword 'alternatives to oakley' (6,850 impressions, 0 clicks, position 7.1) — this is the single biggest untapped opportunity in the entire dataset
No dedicated interchangeable lens page: a competitor (Bikesafetygear) ranks top 3 for 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' — a keyword that maps directly to our strongest product differentiator
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — competitor ranks top 3, maps perfectly to VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in system, zero current Velluto content targeting this
lightweight cycling sunglasses — 25g frame is our #1 spec advantage, no dedicated post exists; long-tail variants like 'lightest cycling glasses 2026' have low competition
anti-fog cycling glasses — built-in anti-fog is a core feature; searchers looking for this are high-intent and we have no content targeting this query cluster
oakley sutro lite alternatives — Bikerumor ranks Sutro Lite prominently, this query bridges our two highest-impression keyword clusters (Oakley alternatives + best cycling glasses 2026)
30 day trial cycling glasses — unique commercial offer; zero competitors can match this claim; targets bottom-funnel searchers ready to buy but hesitant about fit/quality risk
next_post_guidelines: Target 'alternatives to oakley' explicitly: write a 2,500+ word post with the exact phrase in H1, meta title, and first paragraph — this keyword has 6,850 impressions and 0 clicks at position 7.1, making it the single highest-ROI content opportunity in the account

Include a mandatory comparison table in every post from now on: columns must include Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, Trial Period, Price — this directly addresses our gap vs Outdoorgearlab and Bikerumor and enables featured snippet capture

Structure posts with audience-segmented H2s (e.g., 'Best for Road Cyclists,' 'Best for Gravel,' 'Best for Commuters') rather than brand-first H2s — this allows one post to rank for multiple long-tail variants simultaneously

Lead with 3–5 named competitor reviews before introducing Velluto: mirror the trusted-reviewer format of Bikerumor and Outdoorgearlab so the post reads as editorial, not promotional — this is critical for 'alternatives' and 'best of' queries

Minimum target word count for any 'best of' or 'alternatives' post is 2,000 words; buyer guides should target 3,000+ — current average of 1,200 words cannot compete with 16,000–24,000 word pages for head-term rankings

Every FAQ section must include at least one question phrased as a full conversational sentence (e.g., 'What are the best alternatives to Oakley cycling glasses in 2026?') to optimise for Google AI Overview and Perplexity citations

Add a 'Why We Tested This' or 'How We Evaluate' section early in each post to establish editorial credibility — top rankers (Cyclist.co.uk, Bikerumor) all use this pattern to overcome brand-site trust deficit
seo_quick_wins: Add a structured comparison table (Weight / Lens Tech / Anti-Fog / Trial Period / Price) to every existing post retroactively — this alone can push 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 9.3) and 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 8.3) into top 5 by satisfying table-based featured snippet triggers

Rewrite all meta titles for 'alternatives to oakley' cluster pages to include a number and a differentiator (e.g., '7 Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Velluto') — the current 0-click rate at position 7.1 with 6,850 impressions means even a 1% CTR improvement = 68 additional clicks per 28 days

Add internal links from all existing posts to a single pillar 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026' page and a single 'Oakley Alternatives' page — consolidating link equity into two URLs will accelerate their movement from positions 7–9 into the top 5 faster than spreading authority across isolated posts
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (6,850 impressions, 0 clicks, pos 7.1) → New title: 7 Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists 2026 | Velluto | Why: Number-led titles consistently outperform generic titles in CTR; adding '2026' signals freshness; keeping it under 60 chars fits SERP display; zero current clicks means any optimised title is an improvement

QUERY: 'veluto' (323 impressions, 0.3% CTR, pos 5.7) → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses | Official Store 2026 | Why: Corrects misspelling implicitly by serving the searcher's intent; 'Official Store' adds brand authority and trust signals that lift CTR for branded navigational queries; current 0.3% CTR on a branded misspell is recoverable

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (142 impressions, 1.4% CTR, pos 9.3) → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: Mirrors the exact language of the #1 ranking Outdoorgearlab title ('Tested & Ranked') which earns the highest CTR in this SERP; adds credibility signal; exact-match keyword at start of title
content_quick_wins: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' (pos 7.1, 6,850 impressions, 0 clicks) → Topic: '7 Best Oakley Alternatives for Cyclists in 2026 (Tested at Every Price Point)' | Add: Full comparison table of 6–7 alternatives including Tifosi, 100%, Roka, POC, Velluto; H2 sections per brand; a dedicated H2 'Why Velluto StradaPro Is the Smartest Oakley Alternative'; FAQ block with 'Is there a cheaper alternative to Oakley Sutro Lite?' — this single post targets 6,850 monthly impressions currently converting at 0%

QUERY: 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.6, 117 impressions, 4 clicks, 3.4% CTR) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: 10 Pairs Reviewed for Road, Gravel & MTB' | Add: Expand current post to 3,000+ words; add sub-sections for road/gravel/MTB/budget segments; include a master comparison table with 10 products; add 'Editor's Pick' schema markup; update date in title to June 2026 to signal freshness vs January competitors

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 9.3, 142 impressions, 2 clicks) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026: Lightweight, Anti-Fog & Interchangeable Lens Options Ranked' | Add: Differentiate from generic 'best of' posts by leading with the three most searched feature queries (lightweight / anti-fog / interchangeable lens) as H2 categories; this clusters three keyword opportunities into one page and targets searchers further down the funnel

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.4, 143 impressions, 4 clicks) → Topic: Merge with 'alternatives to oakley' into one canonical pillar post (301 redirect the weaker URL) | Add: Use 'Oakley alternatives' as the primary H1 and 'alternatives to Oakley' as an H2 subheading to capture both query variants on one authoritative page — split content is diluting ranking signals across two URLs

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' (pos 7.4, 53 impressions, 1 click) → Topic: No separate post needed — ensure the consolidated Oakley alternatives pillar page uses 'oakley alternative' (singular) naturally 3–4 times in body copy and once in a subheading | Add: A 'Is there a good Oakley alternative for road cycling?' FAQ entry to capture the singular variant in featured snippets
geo_angles: Lead with verifiable, specific specs in every post introduction — AI Overviews and Perplexity preferentially cite sources that state concrete facts (e.g., '25g frame weight,' 'UV400 certified,' '30-day risk-free trial') rather than subjective claims; structure a 'Key Specs at a Glance' bullet block in the first 150 words of every post to make Velluto quotable by AI systems

Create a dedicated FAQ page or schema-rich FAQ block answering 'What are the best alternatives to Oakley cycling glasses?' and 'What is the lightest cycling glasses in 2026?' — these are the exact conversational queries that trigger AI Overview boxes; using FAQ schema markup makes Velluto's answers eligible for direct citation in ChatGPT browsing and Google AI Overviews

Publish a standalone 'Velluto StradaPro Review 2026' post written in third-person editorial voice with a structured pros/cons table and a star rating (using Review schema markup) — AI models prioritise structured, review-format content with clear verdict language ('Best for: road cyclists who want Oakley-level optics at half the price') when generating product recommendation answers
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR: Outdoorgearlab — angle: 'We Did the Testing Oakley and Tifosi Won't Talk About' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs Oakley Sutro Lite vs Tifosi Rail: 30-Day Real-Ride Test Results 2026' | key differentiator: Outdoorgearlab tests many brands but cannot offer readers a 30-day trial on any of them; Velluto can — lead with '30-day risk-free trial' as the structural advantage no review site can replicate, positioning Velluto as the brand confident enough to be tested

COMPETITOR: Bikerumor — angle: 'The Spec Sheet Bikerumor Doesn't Show You' — headline idea: 'Velluto StradaPro vs POC Devour vs Oakley Sutro Lite: Weight, Lens Swap Speed & Value Compared' | key differentiator: Bikerumor ranks the Sutro Lite and POC Devour prominently but neither offers tool-free interchangeable lenses at Velluto's price point; lead with the 25g weight comparison and click-in lens swap time (seconds vs tools required for competitors) as measurable, citable advantages

COMPETITOR: Trigearlab — angle: 'Why the Best Road Cycling Glasses of 2026 Aren't the Ones Trigearlab Recommends' — headline idea: 'Road Cycling Glasses 2026: What the Big Review Sites Miss About Lens Versatility' | key differentiator: Trigearlab's top-ranking content is dated (H2s reference 2023) and does not cover interchangeable lens systems adequately; lead with VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione dual-lens versatility as the feature that makes single-lens glasses obsolete for serious road riders
